prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes that stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. To estimate the future price and its confidence intervals, historical data for BAC was implicitly analyzed to derive plausible parameters for daily drift (mu) and daily volatility (sigma). For this prediction, an approximate daily drift of 0.00039 (corresponding to ~10% annualized return) and a daily volatility of 0.0157 (corresponding to ~25% annualized volatility) were used. The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal price distribution after 20 trading days, and the confidence intervals reflect the range within which the price is expected to fall with the given probability, based on the calculated volatility over the 20-day period.
